subject: Add versions of tools used during verification
commit_message:
Add versions of tools used during verification

Issue-ID: INT-1601
Change-Id: I3ff59402627e679a6a5dcdb5a64f2d04d1df09cf
Signed-off-by: Pawel Wieczorek <p.wieczorek2@samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Keeping only symlinks as the markup indicator does not trigger CI on relevant patches (there's no change in symlink). I'll look into this issue

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Keeping only symlinks as the markup indicator does not trigger CI on relevant patches (there's no change in symlink). I'll look into this issue

Gerrit trigger in Jenkins spawns a review job based on a file name pattern (there is no file content heuristics or so), in this case where you have no update to the symlinks it won't trigger.

There are only three options:
+ remove symlinks and add suffix to the readme files
+ trigger review job manually (but i'm afraid it won't still validate files as they won't be in review file list)
+ manually add 'verified' flag but this would have to be the case for any further changes to those files (no automatic verification in CI) which is breaking the idea behind introduced rst/md linters.

Personally I'd go with 1st option although I understand your reasoning behind using symlinks you mentioned in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/109248/5 in reply to my question about symlink.

> Gerrit trigger in Jenkins spawns a review job based on a file name pattern (there is no file content heuristics or so), in this case where you have no update to the symlinks it won't trigger.
> 
> There are only three options:
> + remove symlinks and add suffix to the readme files
> + trigger review job manually (but i'm afraid it won't still validate files as they won't be in review file list)
> + manually add 'verified' flag but this would have to be the case for any further changes to those files (no automatic verification in CI) which is breaking the idea behind introduced rst/md linters.
> 
> Personally I'd go with 1st option although I understand your reasoning behind using symlinks you mentioned in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/109248/5 in reply to my question about symlink.

I guess Sphinx and RDT aren't going anywhere anytime soon. I'll go with (1) as you suggested
